task: Set up the long-term media storage architecture for scaling Blue Life Commons animal images without turning GitHub or Vercel into the bulk image warehouse.
surface: /media-intelligence storage section, R2/S3 storage policy, generated storage manifest, SQL registry schema, environment contract, and storage runbook.
audience: Maintainers, reviewers, contributors, and future platform operators who need a durable image storage plan for thousands to millions of animal media records.
first_read: GitHub stores metadata and review truth; R2/S3-compatible object storage stores pixels; Postgres tracks operational state; Vercel renders the app.

references.label: Cloudflare R2 overview
references.reason: Primary storage provider and public object-store architecture.

references.label: Cloudflare R2 pricing
references.reason: Cost-risk model for storage, operations, and egress.

references.label: GitHub large-file guidance
references.reason: Repo boundary for avoiding bulk image binaries in Git.

keep: GitHub remains the transparent metadata and review ledger.
Object storage is the durable pixel layer.
Vercel remains the app and publishing layer.
Current public pages keep using approved source URLs until owned derivatives exist.
The storage manifest does not download, transform, upload, or sign any media.
avoid: Committing original media libraries to Git.
Switching public routes to owned URLs before derivatives, checksums, and audit rows exist.
Using request-time image transformation as the default scaling path.
direction.signature: A practical scale-control plane: buckets, env vars, SQL, deterministic object keys, generated manifest, and public storage status on the media intelligence page.
system: scripts/build_species_media_storage_manifest.py generates deterministic storage records from approved media.
schema/media-storage-schema.sql defines operational tables for assets, variants, grants, sources, and review events.
lib/media-storage.ts feeds the public storage architecture section.
.env.example records the required public and secret variables without real credentials.

asset_quality.why_premium: The primary asset is operational product infrastructure with a public-facing control surface and source-backed storage decision.

artifacts.notes: Bucket, env, variant, repo-boundary, and migration contract.
31 approved records mapped to 186 planned objects and 155 public variants.
Postgres operational registry schema for media assets, variants, rights grants, sources, and review events.
Public storage architecture section added to the media intelligence page.

checks.name: asset-tier
checks.evidence: Tier A product-proof infrastructure: real approved media records are mapped into storage keys and the public page shows live storage counts.

checks.name: build
checks.evidence: npm run build passed with the known nonfatal Turbopack NFT tracing warning from app/sitemap.ts through lib/content.ts.

checks.name: visual-qa
checks.evidence: Desktop and mobile screenshots show readable storage stats and layer cards without overlap.

checks.name: public-safety
checks.evidence: Built route still renders 31 approved images and zero review-only candidate leakage.

score.notes: Strong architecture and generated migration manifest. Remaining score is reserved for real R2 bucket provisioning and upload/variant workers once credentials are available.

known_risks: Cloudflare account credentials and DNS access are required before buckets and media.bluelifecommons.org can be provisioned.
The current manifest intentionally leaves every record at mirror_pending until actual derivative generation and upload exist.
Future route switching must wait for database rows, checksums, public variant URLs, and review events.
